meta.description: Normalized answer bank for job application automation. Each entry maps a canonical intent to one or more phrasings and a structured answer.

answers.canonical_question: Are you legally authorized to work in the United States?
match_phrases: Are you legally authorized to work in the United States?
Are you authorized to work in the US?
Do you have the legal right to work in the United States?
Are you eligible to work in the United States?
Can you legally work in the US without restriction?
answers.answer_long: Yes, I am a U.S. citizen and am legally authorized to work in the United States without restriction.
answers.notes: Always review — some forms combine this with sponsorship or have unusual phrasing

answers.canonical_question: Do you now or will you in the future require sponsorship?
match_phrases: Do you now or will you in the future require sponsorship?
Will you now or in the future require visa sponsorship?
Do you require employer sponsorship to work in the US?
Are you eligible to work without employer sponsorship?
Will you require sponsorship for employment visa status?
Do you need visa support now or in the future?
answers.answer_long: No, I do not require sponsorship now or in the future. I am a U.S. citizen.
answers.answer_inverted_note: For questions phrased as 'Are you eligible WITHOUT sponsorship?' the answer is Yes
answers.notes: Watch for inverted phrasing. Some forms ask 'can you work WITHOUT sponsorship' (answer: Yes)

answers.canonical_question: What is your citizenship status?
match_phrases: Are you a citizen or permanent resident?

answers.canonical_question: Do you have an active security clearance?
match_phrases: Do you have an active security clearance?
Do you hold a security clearance?
answers.answer_long: I do not currently hold a security clearance but am willing to obtain one if required.

answers.canonical_question: Are you willing to relocate?
match_phrases: Would you be open to relocation?
Can you relocate for this position?
answers.answer_long: I'm open to relocation depending on the role and location.
answers.notes: Depends on specific location — flag for review

answers.canonical_question: What is your preferred work arrangement?
match_phrases: What is your preferred work arrangement?
Are you open to remote work?
Are you open to working in-office?
answers.answer_long: I prefer remote work but am open to hybrid or on-site arrangements depending on the role.

answers.canonical_question: What are your salary expectations?
match_phrases: What is your desired salary range?
answers.answer_long: I'm targeting roles in the $110,000 to $120,000 range, though I'm open to discussing the full compensation package depending on the role, scope, and growth opportunity.
answers.notes: NEVER auto-submit. Always review in context of specific role.

answers.canonical_question: What is your earliest available start date?
match_phrases: What is your earliest available start date?
answers.answer_long: I can be available within approximately two weeks of an accepted offer, though timing is flexible depending on circumstances.
answers.notes: Notice period is approximately 2 weeks. Exact date depends on circumstances.

answers.canonical_question: Are you willing to travel?
match_phrases: What percentage of travel is acceptable?
Can you travel for this role?
answers.answer_long: Yes, limited travel is fine; ideally up to 10-15% depending on the role.

answers.canonical_question: Are you willing to undergo a background check?
match_phrases: Are you willing to undergo a background check?
Do you consent to a background check?
Will you submit to a background investigation?

answers.canonical_question: Are you willing to undergo drug screening?
match_phrases: Are you willing to undergo drug screening?
Do you consent to a drug test?
answers.notes: Preferred answer is No. Review in context — may be a dealbreaker for some roles.

answers.canonical_question: Do you have a non-compete or non-solicitation agreement?
match_phrases: Do you have a non-compete or non-solicitation agreement?
Are you bound by a non-compete?
Are you subject to any restrictive covenants?

answers.canonical_question: Have you previously worked at this company?
match_phrases: Have you previously worked at this company?

answers.canonical_question: How did you hear about this role?
match_phrases: How did you hear about this role?
How did you find this job?
Where did you learn about this opportunity?
How did you hear about us?
answers.answer: LinkedIn / company careers page / recruiter outreach
answers.notes: Select the most accurate option from the dropdown. Defaults vary by actual source.

answers.canonical_question: How many years of experience do you have with [skill]?
match_phrases: How many years of experience do you have with
answers.notes: Resolved dynamically from profile.skills[]. Falls through to skill-aware matcher.

answers.canonical_question: How many years of professional software development experience do you have?
match_phrases: How many years of professional experience do you have?
How many years have you been a software engineer?
answers.answer_long: Approximately 3 years of professional software development experience, plus academic and personal project work dating back to 2019.

answers.canonical_question: Why are you looking for a new role?
match_phrases: Why are you looking for a new role?
Why are you leaving your current position?
Why do you want to leave your current job?
answers.answer_long: I'm looking for a role where I can continue growing as an engineer while taking on larger technical ownership, building impactful products, and working on systems at a broader scale.

answers.canonical_question: Why are you interested in this company?
match_phrases: Why are you interested in this company?
Why do you want to work here?
What attracted you to this role?
Tell us why you'd be a great fit
answers.answer_template: Reference the company's product, mission, engineering culture, or technical challenge; connect that to your background in ownership and delivery; close with why the role matches your next step.
answers.notes: ALWAYS requires customization per company. Never auto-fill with generic text.

answers.canonical_question: What are your strengths?
match_phrases: What do you consider your key strengths?
answers.answer_long: End-to-end ownership, problem solving, backend development, architecture thinking, and translating ambiguous requirements into working solutions.

answers.canonical_question: What is your greatest weakness or area of growth?
match_phrases: What is your area of growth?
What would you like to improve about yourself?
answers.answer_long: I tend to hold myself to a high standard and can spend extra time refining a solution when a faster first version would be sufficient. I've gotten better at balancing quality with iteration speed and aligning effort to the business need.

answers.canonical_question: Describe your ideal work environment.
match_phrases: What kind of work environment do you thrive in?
What is your preferred company culture?
answers.answer_long: A collaborative environment with strong ownership, clear priorities, thoughtful technical discussion, and room to solve real problems without unnecessary process overhead.

answers.canonical_question: Where do you see yourself in 5 years?
match_phrases: Where do you see yourself in 5 years?
What are your long-term career goals?
Where do you see your career heading?
answers.answer_long: I see myself as a more senior engineer with a strong track record of technical ownership, system design, and mentorship, contributing meaningfully to product and engineering direction.

answers.canonical_question: Do you have experience with [skill]?
answers.notes: Cross-reference against skills list in profile.json. Auto-fill Yes for listed skills, flag unknown skills for review.

answers.canonical_question: Have you ever been convicted of a felony?
match_phrases: Have you ever been convicted of a felony?
Do you have a criminal record?
Have you been convicted of a crime?

answers.canonical_question: Are you at least 18 years of age?
match_phrases: Are you at least 18 years of age?

answers.canonical_question: What is your highest level of education?
match_phrases: What is your highest level of education?
What is the highest degree you have earned?
answers.answer_long: Bachelor of Science in Information Technology from University of Central Florida.

answers.canonical_question: What was your GPA?
What year did you graduate?
What is your notice period?
match_phrases: How much notice do you need to give?
When can you start after accepting an offer?
answers.answer_long: Approximately two weeks from acceptance of offer.

answers.canonical_question: Do you have any conflicts of interest?
match_phrases: Do you have any conflicts of interest?
Are you aware of any conflicts of interest?

answers.canonical_question: Do you require any accommodations during the interview process?
match_phrases: Do you need accommodations for the interview?

answers.canonical_question: Do you hold any relevant certifications?
answers.answer_long: I do not currently hold professional certifications, though I am open to pursuing relevant ones.

answers.canonical_question: What languages do you speak?
match_phrases: Do you speak any other languages?
answers.answer_long: English (native fluency).

answers.canonical_question: Were you referred by anyone? If so, who?
match_phrases: Do you know anyone who works here?
answers.notes: Fill with referrer name if applicable.

answers.canonical_question: What size team do you prefer working with?
match_phrases: What size team do you prefer?
How large of a team do you prefer?
answers.answer_long: I work well in small to mid-sized teams (3-10 people) where there is strong collaboration and individual ownership.

answers.canonical_question: Do you have management or leadership experience?
answers.answer_long: I have not formally managed a team, but I have taken ownership of projects, mentored teammates, and contributed to technical decision-making independently.

answers.canonical_question: Do you agree to the terms and conditions?
match_phrases: Do you agree to the terms and conditions?
I certify that the information provided is accurate

answers.canonical_question: Do you acknowledge the Equal Employment Opportunity statement?

behavioral_stories.situation: Stepped into a more independent role on a small engineering team with less direct oversight.
behavioral_stories.action: Handled technical decision-making, supported project execution, and carried backend responsibilities independently.
behavioral_stories.result: Increased trust from leadership, broader ownership of critical systems, and stronger positioning as a go-to backend contributor.

behavioral_stories.situation: Worked on AI and sensor-data efforts where the data was complex and unstructured, needing to become client-usable.
behavioral_stories.action: Interpreted patterns, supported analysis and visualization, and translated technical findings into actionable insight.
behavioral_stories.result: Delivered client-facing analysis that supported decision-making around locomotive maintenance and fault detection.

behavioral_stories.situation: Identified personal and family needs that could be solved with custom software.
behavioral_stories.action: Built personal products and apps from concept and architecture through implementation and refinement, handling every stage independently.
behavioral_stories.result: Delivered functional products demonstrating full-lifecycle ownership and practical architecture skills.

demographic_defaults.veteran_status: I am not a protected veteran

gender_decline: I do not wish to answer
race_decline: Two or more races (not Hispanic or Latino)
veteran_decline: I am not a protected veteran
disability_decline: I do not wish to answer
